title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 August 2024.

items.title: Lego confirms six all-new **Mario Kart** sets are on the way
bullets: Launching on New Year's Day 2025, Lego says "each set has been designed to ensure that anyone can enjoy the joy of the Super Mario universe" and includes interactive elements such as audio and visual effects.

items.title: Digital-only **Xbox Series X** release date announced, pre-orders open
bullets: The new batch of Xbox consoles will be released on 15th October in select countries then releasing in another batch of countries following on 29th October

bullets.text: Xbox Series S 1TB in Robot White - £299.99
Xbox Series X 2TB Galaxy Black Special Edition - £549.99

items.title: New **Xbox** accessibility products revealed, including the **Adaptive Joystick**
bullets: Xbox Adaptive Joystick, 3D Printable Files for Adaptive Thumbstick Toppers, 8BitDo Lite SE 2.4G Wireless Controller, ByoWave Proteus Controller

items.title: **Nintendo** is ending service for another mobile game: Animal Crossing
bullets: A new version of Pocket Camp will launch in November to preserve Save Data

From November 29, 2024 – seven years after Pocket Camp launched – the current app will end service. Players will no longer be able to purchase microtransactions, and the monthly subscription service will also end ahead of this date.

items.title: Three new **Lego Animal Crossing** sets are coming next year
bullets: Lego Animal Crossing Stargazing with Celeste (€9,99/ $9.99 / £8,99)
Lego Animal Crossing Leif’s Caravan & Garden Shop (Price: €29,99/ $29.99 / £24,99)
Lego Animal Crossing Able Sisters Clothing Shop (Price: €39,99/ $39.99 / £34,99)

items.title: **Game of Thrones** star Gwendoline Christie is the narrator of **Civilization VII**
The **Game Awards 2024** date confirmed for December
bullets: The event’s 10th anniversary gala celebration will take place on December 12th
Geoff Keighley's comments about looking ahead "at the next 10 years" have sparked Switch 2 rumours
Response online has been mixed due to the treatment of developers at last year's event ("please wrap it up") and the aggressive amount of advertisements

items.title: ‘There’s going to be more change’: **Phil Spencer** speaks on Indy PS5 news
bullets: Exec claims Xbox console player numbers this year are "as high as they've ever been"

items.title: **Sea of Thieves** has reportedly surpassed one million sales on PS5
bullets: While Xbox hasn’t announced sales figures for the game on PS5, there’s been plenty of evidence to suggest the $40/£40 title has performed strongly since its release in late April.

According to Sony, Sea of Thieves was the most downloaded PS5 game in both the US/Canada and Europe in May. In June, it ranked among the top five downloaded games, and in July it remained in the top ten.
